goal: Add mainline abandon CLI; fix engine.Abandon to write actor-log event so cross-actor sync sees abandoned status

summary.title: mainline abandon CLI + actor-log event for cross-actor visibility
summary.what: Adds `mainline abandon <intent>` CLI command (Daily commands group). Fixes Service.Abandon engine method which previously only updated the local draft file — no IntentAbandonedEvent was written to the actor log, so cross-actor sync would never see the abandon and the intent would silently stay proposed in everyone elses view. New behaviour branches on prior state: drafting deletes the draft files (local-only, nothing public to clean); sealed_local/proposed appends IntentAbandonedEvent to the actor log and auto-publishes. Signature changed from `error` to `(*AbandonResult, error)` carrying PriorStatus / EventID / Published / Warning. 4 existing test callsites updated. 2 new tests covering both branches.
summary.why: Surfaced by my own closed PR #35 — the flake-fix intent was redundant because z2z23n0 shipped #34 first. There was no way to mark the corresponding mainline intent as abandoned: the engine method existed but was incomplete (local-only) and no CLI surface existed. A proposed intent that someone abandoned should not show up in `mainline list-proposals` or as a phase-1 conflict candidate forever. The user explicitly flagged this gap when I closed the PR ("proposed should be able to close or delete?"), and the answer is yes — the data model has supported it since rc1, only the wiring was missing.
summary.user_goal: Be able to drop an in-flight intent (drafting, sealed_local, proposed) and have the entire team see the change without manual intervention.

decisions.point: Single verb (abandon) vs separate verbs (drop / cancel / abandon)
decisions.rationale: Same user mental model ("Im done with this intent"), different mechanics under the hood. New verbs are expensive (the user pushed back twice on adding `mainline adopt`). Branch on prior status inside the engine; CLI is the same call.

decisions.point: Drafting state: delete files vs write tombstone
decisions.rationale: Drafts are local-only — never published, no public observers. A tombstone (status=abandoned in the draft file) would persist a useless artifact and complicate draft directory listing. Cleaner to remove the entire footprint when there was nothing public to advertise.

Cross-actor visibility is the whole point. If we deferred to next mainline publish run, the user would have to remember an extra step or wait for the freshness window. Failure to publish becomes a Warning — non-fatal — so the local state still updates and the user can retry with mainline publish.

IntentSupersededEvent is in the data model but adding the CLI semantics (which intent supersedes which? validate the new intent exists? write the link both ways?) is a separate scope. The two cases that the user case from PR #35 is more "abandoned, with reason" than "superseded by independent parallel work" — the friends PR was not consciously a replacement. Keep abandon clean; add supersede when a real use case demands it.

decisions.chose: non-fatal warning + suggest mainline publish to retry
decisions.rationale: Local state changes succeed regardless. The actor log event is written; pushing it is best-effort. This matches seal --submits warning pattern when push fails — failure modes should not block local progress.

rejected.alternative: Add a separate `mainline drop` for drafts and `mainline cancel` for sealed
rejected.reason: Two CLI verbs for one user concept. Increases surface area without semantic value.

rejected.alternative: Write a tombstone file instead of deleting the draft
rejected.reason: Persists a useless artifact — drafting was never publicly observable, so theres nothing to cite as gone.
Would block local state on transient network issues. Mirroring seal --submits non-fatal pattern keeps local-vs-remote concerns separated.
Non-trivial scope (link semantics, validate new-intent existence, two-way pointer). Out of scope; ship abandon clean first.

risks: AbandonResult signature is a breaking change for any downstream caller of Service.Abandon. The internal codebase is the only consumer (4 test callsites updated); no external packages exist yet. Risk only matters if/when the engine becomes a public package.

For proposed intents that are in fact already-merged on another actor, abandoning would create an event chronologically AFTER the merge note. Sync replays in chronological order so the LATER event wins — abandoning a recently-merged intent could revert its visible status from merged back to abandoned. Mitigated by ValidateStateTransition rejecting merged→abandoned at the source actor; cross-actor races where one actor merges while another simultaneously abandons remain a theoretical edge case worth documenting if it ever bites.
No undo: abandon is terminal per the state machine. Documented in CLI help; matches the intentional-immutable design.
followups: Add `mainline supersede <old-intent> --by <new-intent>` for the case where a new intent explicitly replaces an old one (vs the abandon semantics for "just dropping it"). Same data model already supports it.

Consider surfacing abandoned intents in `mainline gaps` rescue suggestions — when an uncovered commit appears, an abandoned intent on the same files is a hint for how to choose between backfill and skip.
Doctor check for stranded proposed intents older than N days with no merge progress — auto-suggest abandon. Aspirational, low priority.

architectural_claims: Service.Abandon writes an IntentAbandonedEvent to the actor log for sealed_local/proposed states so cross-actor sync sees the abandon.
Drafting-state abandon is delete-only — drafting was never publicly observable so there is nothing to advertise as gone.
mainline abandon CLI is a Daily-group command; abandoning is a normal flow action rather than a recovery operation.
behavioral_changes: mainline abandon <intent> is a new top-level CLI command.
Service.Abandon signature changed from `error` to `(*AbandonResult, error)` — carries prior status, event id, publish state, warning.
Abandoning a proposed intent now propagates to all teammates clones via the actor log + auto-push, instead of silently staying proposed in their view.
Drafting-state abandon now deletes the draft entirely; was previously a tombstone-style update.
